title: Daily Market Digest
subtitle: April 14, 2026: Market Rallies on Geopolitical Optimism

items.headline: U.S. Stocks Rally as Hopes for Renewed U.S.-Iran Talks Emerge
items.summary: Wall Street experienced a significant rally on Monday, April 13, recovering losses incurred from the U.S.-Iran conflict. The positive sentiment was largely driven by expectations of a possible second round of talks between the United States and Iran aimed at resolving the ongoing war. Technology stocks led the upward movement across major indices.

items.headline: Oracle Soars on AI Infrastructure Strength
items.summary: Oracle Corporation ($ORCL) was a top gainer, surging on Monday as investors recognized its strengthening position in the AI infrastructure race. The company reported an extraordinary increase in its remaining performance obligations and deepened its partnership with Nvidia.

items.headline: ServiceNow Climbs Amid Broader Tech Gains
items.summary: ServiceNow ($NOW) posted significant gains, reflecting the strong performance of technology stocks fueled by positive market sentiment and the broader rally.

items.headline: Goldman Sachs Dips Despite Strong Earnings
items.summary: Goldman Sachs ($GS) shares dropped despite the investment bank reporting better-than-expected quarterly profits. The decline was attributed to disappointment on Wall Street regarding lower-than-expected fixed-income revenue.

items.headline: Lipocine Plummets on Trial Results
items.summary: Lipocine Inc. ($LPCN) experienced a sharp decline after reporting its topline safety and efficacy results for LPCN 1154, a treatment for Postpartum Depression.

items.headline: Geopolitical Optimism Fuels Market Rally
items.summary: Hopes for renewed talks between the U.S. and Iran regarding the ongoing conflict, despite initial failed peace talks, have significantly boosted market sentiment, leading to a broad rally across major U.S. indices on Monday.

items.headline: Q1 Earnings Season Kicks Off with Mixed Bank Results
items.summary: The first-quarter earnings season is underway, with major banks like JPMorgan Chase ($JPM), Wells Fargo ($WFC), and Citigroup ($C) set to report today, April 14. Goldman Sachs ($GS) already saw its stock drop despite beating profit expectations, due to disappointing fixed-income revenue.

items.headline: Oil Prices Retreat Amid Diplomacy Hopes
items.summary: Brent crude prices fell below $100 per barrel on Tuesday, paring earlier gains, as renewed optimism for U.S.-Iran talks eased concerns over supply disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz.

items.headline: China's Export Growth Slows Amid Iran War Uncertainty
items.summary: China reported a significant slowdown in export growth for March, rising only 2.5%, well below analysts' estimates. This deceleration is attributed to the Iran war's impact on global demand and supply chains.

items.headline: AI Sector Sees Clear Winners and Losers in 2026
items.summary: The artificial intelligence boom continues to shape market performance, with companies like Oracle ($ORCL) significantly benefiting from the demand for AI infrastructure. Conversely, some software stocks, such as Atlassian ($TEAM), are facing challenges and declines due to investor fears of AI disruption.
